mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the unemployment rate above 3.8% signals a tightening labor market that compresses wage growth and reduces the attractiveness of equities, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in industrial production above 109.5 signals a strengthening demand cycle that boosts equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in strengthening periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in housing starts below 1.2 million signals a weakening supply cycle that reduces the attractiveness of equities, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in weakening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real disposable personal income above 519 billion signals a strengthening demand cycle that boosts equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in strengthening periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in payroll employment above 151 million signals a strengthening labor market that compresses wage growth and reduces the attractiveness of equities, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in strengthening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the consumer price index above 255.5 signals a strengthening monetary cycle that boosts inflation expectations and reduces the attractiveness of equities, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in strengthening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real GDP above 18910 billion signals a strengthening demand cycle that boosts equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in strengthening periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
